* 32-bit header (as described above)
* 8-byte agent ID (aid): identifies the requesting agent

* 8-bit response type (rt): specifies the type of response (e.g., success, failure, or pending)
* 16-bit response code (rc): provides additional information about the response
* 8-byte transaction ID (tid): identifies the transaction associated with the request

The agent can then use the transaction ID to poll the dispatcher for the status of the request or to retrieve the response payload.

5. Returns control to the requesting agent, which can then retrieve the response from the response buffer.

* SID 0x0001: Memory Allocation (returns a pointer to a newly allocated memory block)

Agents can extend this set of services by registering custom service handlers with the kernel, which will be invoked when the corresponding SID is received in an IR.
